discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape with SMH +2% and VIX -5%, led by mega-cap tech and semis. Defense name AVAV giving back gains after a huge multi-day run; Texas Roadhouse weak against a cyclical-up tape suggesting name-specific pressure. Trump post on Walmart lowering prices and Saudi oil cut is the notable macro cross-current.

notable_index_moves: SMH +2% and QQQ +1.4% with AVGO +3.7%, TSM +4% — semis leading
TSLA +6.7% on heavy volume — EV story flagged in news
XLV -1.1% and XLP -1% laggards; defensive rotation out
Oil sector flat/down (XLE -0.17%) consistent with Saudi price cut headline

discoveries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Profit-taking pullback after a sharp multi-day run in a defense name
discoveries.thesis: AVAV ran ~28% over five sessions into today; a pullback of this magnitude on no fresh catalyst looks like mechanical profit-taking rather than a thesis change. Volume only modestly above average and ITA (defense ETF) is actually up on the day, so this is name-specific give-back not sector-wide unwind. Magnitude looks proportional to how stretched the move was.
discoveries.what_confirms: No 8-K, no analyst downgrade, no peer weakness (KTOS/RKLB) confirms this is unwind of the run rather than news. If AVAV stabilizes within a day or two while ITA holds up, profit-taking read is validated.
discoveries.what_kills: An 8-K or news release later today (contract loss, secondary offering, insider selling filing) would flip this to catalyst-driven and potentially UNDERDONE to the downside. A secondary offering announcement after a run of this size is a classic pattern to watch for.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Defense small/mid-caps that rip 25%+ in a week on no fundamental change frequently see 5-10% single-day give-backs; check for a follow-on equity raise as the most common actual catalyst.
discoveries.catalyst: No identified catalyst; likely profit-taking after 5-day surge
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals is empty {} — no 8-K, no earnings scheduled; move context is the prior 5-day rip
research_pointers: Check for after-hours 8-K filing or secondary offering announcement
Compare against KTOS, RKLB, MRCY drone/defense peers today
Review what drove the initial 28% five-day run — earnings? contract? sector rotation?
Insider Form 4 filings in last 48 hours

discoveries.name: Texas Roadhouse, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Restaurant name weak against a risk-on cyclical tape
discoveries.thesis: TXRH is down while XLY is up nearly 1%, so this is name-specific not sector. No 8-K, no earnings imminent per catalyst_signals. Trump's post about Walmart cutting ground beef prices ~15% is a potential thread — beef is TXRH's core input cost, so lower beef could actually be BULLISH for margins, not bearish. Cannot construct a confident directional read from available evidence.
discoveries.what_confirms: N/A — this is genuinely opaque without more data
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown; possible read-through from Trump/Walmart beef price commentary or an unreported analyst action
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; move is counter-tape and counter-sector
research_pointers: Check for analyst downgrade/estimate cut today (Stephens, Wedbush cover TXRH)
Same-store sales data / OpenTable proxy for June
Any read-through from DRI, CAKE, EAT price action today
Beef input cost commentary — is Trump/Walmart beef price pressure a margin positive?

discoveries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semi-equipment small-cap down sharply against a screaming semi tape
discoveries.thesis: VECO down while SMH +2%, TSM +4%, AVGO +3.7% is a striking divergence. Stock is down ~23% over five sessions with no 8-K and no earnings imminent — this is either late-cycle capitulation on an already-broken chart or specific customer/OEM concern (Veeco sells to memory/advanced packaging). Against this semi tape, if there's no company-specific bad news, the disconnect creates asymmetric bounce potential. Low volume (0.62x) on today's drop suggests forced/passive selling rather than fresh negative info.
discoveries.what_confirms: If peers ACLS, ONTO, KLAC hold up while VECO continues to bleed, and no 8-K appears, the divergence is stretched enough to fade. Confirm no pending customer concentration disclosure (memory capex cuts).
discoveries.what_kills: An 8-K, preannouncement, or news of a major customer (Samsung, SK Hynix, TSMC) cutting orders would validate the underperformance as fundamental. Also: if ASML/AMAT commentary this week flagged advanced packaging or MOCVD weakness, VECO drop is justified.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Small-cap semi-equipment names that decouple sharply from SMH on low volume with no 8-K are often mispriced — the divergence tends to compress within 1-2 weeks unless a customer-specific catalyst emerges.
discoveries.catalyst: No identified catalyst; possible customer concentration or memory capex fear
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; volume BELOW average on a down day is atypical for real bad-news selling
research_pointers: Check Veeco's largest customers and any recent memory/HBM capex commentary
Compare to ACLS, ONTO, KLIC, AEHR — other small-cap semi equipment
Options skew and any unusual put activity last week
Upcoming earnings date and pre-announce window

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Lockheed Martin to buy Ultra Maritime for $3.45B (anti-submarine tech)

secondary_opportunities.relationship: sympathy - defense M&A premium for unmanned/specialty defense
sympathy - specialty defense small/mid-cap could get takeout premium

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Saudi Arabia largest oil price cut in decades; Trump amplifying

secondary_opportunities.relationship: customer - lower diesel/energy input helps restaurant margins marginally

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump touts Walmart cutting ground beef price ~15% and other prices; frames as administration achievement
trump_signals.note: If beef price relief is real and sustained, it's a margin tailwind for beef-heavy restaurants like TXRH and LongHorn (DRI) — potentially why TXRH weakness today is counterintuitive/mispriced. Grocers (KR, ACI) face marginally negative optics if forced to match. WMT positioning as 'patriotic' aligns them with administration — reduces regulatory/tariff overhang risk.

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump amplifying Saudi oil price cut as administration win
trump_signals.note: Confirms White House is comfortable with lower oil — bearish setup for E&P mid-caps, bullish for transports and consumer discretionary. Consistent with XLE flat while XLY up today.

ai_announcements.headline: Microsoft lays off nearly 5,000 employees across Xbox, commercial sales
ai_announcements.summary: Microsoft cut ~4,800 roles (2.1% of workforce), citing AI-driven efficiency; latest in a running series of AI-linked layoffs in 2026.

ai_announcements.headline: Vercel CEO on splitting models from agents
ai_announcements.summary: Rauch argues production agent builders will pick models on price/performance, commoditizing the model layer beneath the agent layer.

ai_announcements.headline: Apple iOS 27 beta adds Siri pace/expressivity customization
ai_announcements.summary: Incremental UX addition as Apple rebuilds Siri around generative AI.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: None of the moving stocks in the market_data (AVAV defense, VECO semi cap equipment, EXTR networking, TXRH restaurants) map cleanly to today's AI announcements. AVAV -7.4% after +28% five-day run looks like profile-taking/defense-sector news, not AI. VECO -23% over five days is a semi-cap equipment story (likely a specific customer/guidance issue), not tied to any headline here. EXTR +5% and TXRH -4% have no plausible mechanism to today's AI news flow. No actionable AI-driven mispricing identified tonight.
